scope.claim_limit: Public urban KITTI seq05 check with more city traffic than seq00/07; not a substitute for dedicated high-dynamic multi-beam benchmarks.

id_lio.note: Indexed-point dynamic scan-to-map: pseudo-occupancy dynamic detection and delayed removal; no imu.csv so constant-velocity fallback. dynamic/frame=455.320174 corr/frame=3121.945310 removed_sum/frame=289929.269830

rf_lio_default.note: RF-LIO removal-first scan-to-map: adaptive multi-resolution range-image foreground removal before indexed dynamic scan-to-map; no imu.csv so constant-velocity fallback. removal_first/frame=272.869975 candidates/frame=272.869975 kept/frame=6727.130025 backend_dynamic/frame=424.967403 corr/frame=2859.649402 backend_removed_sum/frame=596646.650489

rf_lio_conservative.note: RF-LIO removal-first scan-to-map: adaptive multi-resolution range-image foreground removal before indexed dynamic scan-to-map; no imu.csv so constant-velocity fallback. removal_first/frame=221.851503 candidates/frame=221.851503 kept/frame=6778.148497 backend_dynamic/frame=431.249909 corr/frame=2876.451648 backend_removed_sum/frame=601685.323071

conclusion: On public KITTI Odometry seq05 full (urban driving), both dynamic paths stay active (ID-LIO dynamic/frame 455, RF-LIO removal_first/frame 273). Default RF-LIO RPE is 0.961% vs ID-LIO 0.712% (+35.0% relative) and KISS-ICP 0.617%. Conservative RF removal changes RPE by +0.5% vs default RF-LIO. This records mechanism behavior on a more dynamic public KITTI sequence but does not replace dedicated high-dynamic multi-beam benchmarks for manuscript-level dynamic-scene claims.
